channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EFCC nabs Cameroonian suspect linked to cross-border ATM fraud scheme in Lagos
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) don arrest one Cameroonian businessman, Bekono Eric, wey dem accuse say he take part for one large-scale bank fraud wey happen for Lagos. According ...

item.title: Downing Street Reaffirms UK's Sovereignty Over Falkland Islands Amid US 'Review' Report
item.description: Following reports of a potential US review regarding the sovereignty of the Falkland Islands, Downing Street has reiterated that the islands' sovereignty remains with the UK. A Pentagon email, reporte...

item.title: Sekhukhune look to recover form while Marumo Gallants fight relegation
item.description: Sekhukhune United are back in action under the guidance of interim coaches Paulus Masehe and Andre Arendse after Eric Tinkler's departure. Their first match under the new leadership ended in a heavy d...

item.title: UST-B Completes Perfect Run in Rising Stars Cup Division 2 Group Stage
item.description: The University of Santo Tomas-B (UST-B) girls' volleyball team showcased an outstanding performance in the Rising Stars Cup Division 2, completing the group stage with a flawless record. Demonstrating...

item.title: Lucio Co’s Acquisition of PrimeWater Faces Operational and Service Challenges
item.description: San Jose del Monte in Bulacan continues to face a worsening water crisis despite the recent acquisition of PrimeWater by Lucio Co's Crystal Bridges. The takeover has yet to result in meaningful improv...

item.title: Alberta introduces streamlined teacher certification pathways to address growing classroom demand
item.description: The Alberta government has announced a new certification framework aimed at addressing a growing shortage of teachers as student enrolment continues to rise. Officials estimate that an additional 80,0...

item.title: Trump States Iran Will Make Offer To Meet U.S. Demands in Upcoming Peace Talks
item.description: In a recent phone interview with Reuters, U.S. President Donald Trump revealed that Iran intends to make an offer aimed at meeting U.S. demands, though he did not specify the details of the proposal. ...

item.title: Joe Abah Supports Increased Allowances for Civil Servants, Says It Won't Affect Economy
item.description: Joe Abah, a Nigerian public governance expert, has praised the Federal Government's decision to increase peculiar allowances and other welfare benefits for civil servants. He spoke on Channels Televis...

item.title: Tracy Shaw shares breast cancer diagnosis and upcoming treatment plan
item.description: Actress Tracy Shaw, best known for her role as Maxine Peacock in Coronation Street, has announced that she has been diagnosed with breast cancer at the age of 52. She shared the news in an Instagram v...

item.title: Angélique Kidjo releases 19th album ‘Hope’ with global stars and tribute to her late mother
item.description: Five-time Grammy winner Angélique Kidjo don drop her 19th studio album titled ‘Hope’, and the project carry plenty big names from across the global music scene. The 16-track album, wey she say take ab...

item.title: Fuzile wins a tactical junior lightweight fight against Gingqi
item.description: In what many hoped would be a thrilling showdown, the junior lightweight fight between Azinga Fuzile and Asanda Gingqi at the packed Orient Theatre in KuGompo City turned out to be more strategic than...

item.title: Bellarmine Mugabe and co-accused appear at Alexandra Magistrate’s Court over attempted murder charges
item.description: Bellarmine Mugabe, the son of the late Zimbabwean president Robert Mugabe, along with Tobias Matonhodze, has returned to the Alexandra Magistrate’s Court facing serious charges of attempted murder. Th...

item.title: B.C. Court Allows RCMP to Dispose of Evidence from Robert Pickton Investigation
item.description: A British Columbia Supreme Court justice has ruled in favour of the RCMP's application to dispose of approximately 15,000 pieces of evidence seized during the investigation into serial killer Robert P...

item.title: Daily Feng Shui Insights from Master Hanz Cua for April 25, 2026
item.description: Master Hanz Cua, a well-known feng shui expert in the Philippines, offers his daily predictions and guidance for April 25, 2026. According to his insights, specific directions, colors, and arrangement...

item.title: Assam Lynching Case: 20 Individuals Receive Life Sentences
item.description: A court in Assam, India, has sentenced 20 individuals to life imprisonment for their involvement in a 2018 mob lynching incident. The case, which drew widespread condemnation, involved the brutal kill...

item.title: Religious Leaders Call for Prayer Amid Security Challenges in Nigeria
item.description: For Jos, Imams wey dey part of the Association of Jumma’at Mosque Imams don raise alarm say Nigeria security system no dey work well, as e don fail for several areas. Dr Yusuf Abdullahi Yusuf, Assista...

item.title: Carlos Alcaraz withdraws from 2026 French Open due to wrist injury
item.description: Spain’s tennis sensation Carlos Alcaraz, currently ranked world number two, has announced his withdrawal from the 2026 French Open owing to a persistent wrist injury. The young athlete, who had been a...

item.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Apologizes to Tumbler Ridge Community for Not Reporting Shooter's ChatGPT Account
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has issued a public apology to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, following a devastating mass shooting in February 2026. The shooter, 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootse...

item.title: Lesufi Denies Docket Interference Claims at Madlanga Commission
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi has strongly refuted claims made at the Madlanga Commission suggesting he requested J50 warrants of arrest or exerted political pressure to access sensitive case dockets...

item.title: White House photo criticised for diminishing visibility of champion women’s tennis team
item.description: A video produced by The Guardian features commentary from Australia picture editor Carly Earl, who examines controversy surrounding an official White House photograph celebrating a championship women’...
